title: Chilli con Carne

overview.description: A rich, slow-simmered beef chilli with kidney beans, tomatoes and warming spices. A perfect batch-cooking meal that freezes exceptionally well.

ingredients: 2 × 400g tins kidney beans, drained and rinsed
method: Heat the vegetable oil in a large saucepan or casserole over a medium-high heat.
Brown the beef mince thoroughly, breaking it into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the onions, carrots and celery and cook for around 10 minutes until softened.
Stir in the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the tomato purée and cook for 2 minutes.
Stir in the cumin, smoked paprika, coriander, oregano, chilli powder and cayenne (if using) and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the chopped tomatoes, beef stock, Worcestershire sauce and brown sugar.
Bring to a gentle simmer and cook uncovered for around 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
Add the kidney beans and continue cooking for another 20–30 minutes until the sauce has thickened.
Season to taste with salt and black pepper.
Finish with freshly chopped coriander before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Although inspired by Mexican flavours, the bean-filled version of chilli is most closely associated with Tex-Mex home cooking.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to balance warming spices, develop flavour through slow simmering and cook a meal that's ideal for batch freezing.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: One of the best batch-cooking recipes in the collection. It's economical, family-friendly and keeps brilliantly in the freezer.
chapman-tips: Cook the mince until properly browned before adding the vegetables.
A long, gentle simmer develops a richer flavour than adding more spices.
Adjust the heat at the table rather than making the whole pot too spicy for the children.
This recipe is even better the following day.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in meal-sized portions for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Serve with baked potatoes, nachos, tacos or use as a filling for burritos.
